questions.q: Kasturba Gandhi was the wife of which leader of the Indian freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Kasturba Gandhi was the wife and lifelong companion of Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi, the Mahatma.

questions.q: By what affectionate name was Kasturba Gandhi widely known?
questions.explanation: Kasturba Gandhi was affectionately called 'Ba', a Gujarati term of respect meaning mother.

questions.q: In which year did Kasturba Gandhi die?
questions.explanation: Kasturba Gandhi died in 1944 while in British detention.

questions.q: Where was Kasturba Gandhi held in detention at the time of her death?
questions.explanation: Kasturba Gandhi died in 1944 while detained at the Aga Khan Palace in Pune.

questions.q: Kasturba Gandhi was repeatedly subjected to which experience during the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Kasturba Gandhi took part in many satyagraha campaigns and was repeatedly imprisoned for her participation.

questions.q: During which mass movement was Kasturba Gandhi detained when she died?
questions.explanation: She was detained during the Quit India movement and died at the Aga Khan Palace in 1944.

questions.q: Kasturba Gandhi participated in which form of resistance championed by her husband?
questions.explanation: Kasturba took part in numerous satyagraha campaigns, the non-violent resistance developed by Gandhi.

questions.q: Kasturba Gandhi is remembered chiefly as a participant in which broader cause?
questions.explanation: Kasturba Gandhi is remembered as a steadfast participant in India's struggle for independence.

questions.q: Kasturba and Mahatma Gandhi shared which kind of relationship over their lives?
questions.explanation: Kasturba was Gandhi's wife and his lifelong companion through his public and private struggles.

questions.q: The Aga Khan Palace, where Kasturba Gandhi died, is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The Aga Khan Palace, where Kasturba was detained and died in 1944, is in Pune.

questions.q: In which city was Kasturba born in 1869?
questions.explanation: Kasturba was born in Porbandar in present-day Gujarat in 1869, the same coastal town as Gandhi.

questions.q: What was Kasturba's maiden surname before her marriage to Gandhi?
questions.explanation: Kasturba was born Kasturbai Kapadia, the daughter of a Porbandar merchant.

questions.q: Kasturba and Gandhi were married while they were still in what stage of life?
questions.explanation: Following the custom of the time, Kasturba and Gandhi were married as children, both around the age of thirteen.

questions.q: Kasturba played an active role in Gandhi's early campaigns in which country before the family returned to India?
questions.explanation: Kasturba joined Gandhi's satyagraha struggles in South Africa, where she too courted arrest, before returning to India.

questions.q: During the South African struggle, Kasturba was imprisoned for protesting against a law affecting which institution?
options: The legal recognition of Indian marriages
questions.explanation: Kasturba was jailed in South Africa during the campaign against a ruling that invalidated Indian marriages.

questions.q: Kasturba was associated with constructive work at which of Gandhi's residential communities in India?
questions.explanation: Kasturba lived and worked at Gandhi's ashrams, including the Sabarmati Ashram near Ahmedabad.

questions.q: Kasturba devoted much of her social work to the welfare of which groups?
questions.explanation: Kasturba worked for the welfare and education of women and children as part of Gandhi's constructive programme.

questions.q: When Gandhi was arrested at the start of the Quit India movement in 1942, what did Kasturba do?
options: She stepped in to address a planned public meeting and was arrested
questions.explanation: After Gandhi's arrest in 1942 Kasturba prepared to address a meeting in his place and was herself taken into custody.

questions.q: How many sons did Kasturba and Mahatma Gandhi have together?
questions.explanation: Kasturba and Gandhi had four sons: Harilal, Manilal, Ramdas, and Devdas.

questions.q: Kasturba's marriage to Gandhi lasted for roughly how long, ending only with her death?
questions.explanation: Married as children in the early 1880s, Kasturba and Gandhi were together for more than sixty years until her death in 1944.

questions.q: Which fund was set up after Kasturba's death to continue work in her name for the upliftment of women and children?
questions.explanation: The Kasturba Gandhi National Memorial Trust was established to carry forward welfare work in rural India in her memory.

questions.q: Kasturba shared Gandhi's commitment to which everyday practice of self-reliance?
questions.explanation: Like Gandhi, Kasturba embraced a life of simplicity and the spinning of khadi as part of the freedom movement's ideals.

questions.q: In Gandhi's South African phase, what was a notable early disagreement between Kasturba and Gandhi over household duties at Phoenix Settlement?
options: Over paying for her children's tuition
Over cleaning the chamber pots of all residents, regardless of caste
questions.explanation: Gandhi recounts in his autobiography a sharp early clash when he expected Kasturba to clean the chamber pots of all household members, including a Christian clerk of untouchable origin.

questions.q: Which of Gandhi's close secretaries also died in detention at the Aga Khan Palace around the same period as Kasturba?
questions.explanation: Gandhi's longtime secretary Mahadev Desai died at the Aga Khan Palace in 1942, before Kasturba's death there in 1944.

questions.q: In the South African satyagraha, Kasturba was among the women who crossed which provincial border to court arrest in 1913?
options: From Cape Colony to Orange Free State
questions.explanation: Kasturba was part of the group of women who illegally crossed between Transvaal and Natal in the 1913 satyagraha and were imprisoned.

questions.q: Kasturba and Gandhi were both born in 1869 in which princely-state region of western India?
questions.explanation: Both Kasturba and Gandhi were born in 1869 in Porbandar, in the Kathiawar region of Gujarat.

questions.q: Kasturba's eldest son, with whom she had a sometimes troubled relationship, was named what?
questions.explanation: Harilal, the eldest of the four sons, had a famously strained relationship with his parents.

questions.q: During the 1922 Bardoli-area and earlier campaigns, what was a recurring role Kasturba took on while Gandhi was imprisoned?
options: Guiding and steadying the ashram community
questions.explanation: When Gandhi was jailed, Kasturba often helped hold together the ashram community and continued constructive work.

questions.q: Kasturba's life and her death in detention are often cited as an example of which aspect of the freedom struggle?
options: The participation and sacrifice of women
questions.explanation: Kasturba is remembered as a symbol of the active participation and sacrifice of Indian women in the freedom movement.

questions.q: Kasturba was first imprisoned during Gandhi's South African phase, an experience she shared even though she had had little of which?
questions.explanation: Kasturba had little formal schooling, yet she courageously courted imprisonment alongside Gandhi from the South African struggle onward.
